findings.title: e2e real-number test fails: getBrandId uses wrong table 'app_user_org_membership' (actual: 'membership')
findings.resolution: Fixed in commit 709cb2c on branch feat/analytics-api-dashboard. Line 38 now reads: JOIN membership m ON m.organization_id = o.id. Source fix confirmed: grep for 'app_user_org_membership' returns zero matches (EXIT 1). 'membership' table confirmed in db/migrations/0003_workspace.sql:49-53 with organization_id and app_user_id columns.
findings.evidence: ✓  2 [chromium] › e2e/realized-revenue.spec.ts:116:5 › realized-revenue card shows the real formatted amount after seeding a finalized ledger row (6.0s) — real ₹1,234 M1 reconciling number rendered and confirmed

findings.title: formatMoneyDisplay uses Number(decimalString) for Intl.NumberFormat — display-only, precision-safe for M1 range
findings.detail: Number(decimalString) is applied to a string like '1234.50' composed from bigint integer division. No parseFloat, no /100 inline. Precision is safe for typical monetary amounts within Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER / 100. Not a D-7 violation (the prohibition is parseFloat and /100 on the raw minor-unit value). Noted for completeness.

negative_control.confirmed_red_on_removal: Test '3. isolation negative-control > current_user is brain_app' asserts current_user='brain_app' AND is_superuser=false. If run under superuser (brain), the is_superuser=false assertion would FAIL. The cross-brand isolation test passes under brain_app because RLS ENABLE+FORCE scopes brand_id to BRAND_B when GUC is set to BRAND_B, making BRAND_A rows invisible. Running the same test under superuser would bypass RLS and potentially return BRAND_A data.
negative_control.evidence: Test 3 suite: 4/4 PASS; current_user assertion confirmed brain_app not brain (prior FULL run; unchanged)
